dependencyEdges.note: Permanent amphitheaters are large built civic venues with durable seating and circulation infrastructure, so organized construction capability is intrinsic to the scoped technology.

name: Water Clock Timekeeping
description: Simple clepsydrae and calibrated vessels used to measure intervals or mark time in courts, religious practice, astronomy, and regulated work periods.
region: Athens, Greece; earlier Egyptian and Babylonian lineages

dependencyEdges.note: The simplest clepsydrae were small bowls or vessels with controlled openings; pottery supplies a practical vessel-making substrate without implying that all water clocks were ceramic.

scopeNote: Covers basic water-clock timekeeping and interval timing. It deliberately excludes Ctesibius-style regulated feedback clocks and later mechanized clockworks.

name: Qanat Water Supply
description: Gently sloping underground galleries with vertical shafts that tap aquifers and deliver groundwater by gravity to settlements and fields.
region: Iran / ancient Persia; later spread across arid Asia and North Africa

dependencyEdges.note: Qanats tap underground water sources and belong to a family of earlier groundwater supply practices.
A gently sloping tunnel system over kilometres requires alignment and gradient control.
Gravity-fed underground channels are the defining hydraulic mechanism of qanat water supply.
